summary: Founded in 1960, one of Nigeria's oldest and largest agro-allied FMCG companies, best known for its Golden Penny brand; delisted from the Nigerian Exchange in January 2025 after majority shareholder Excelsior Shipping acquired all minority shares.
offering: Sugarcane cultivation and refining, flour milling, and production/distribution of pasta, noodles, margarine, vegetable oils, and refined sugar under the Golden Penny flagship brand and its agro-allied division.

pricing.notes: Standard retail/wholesale FMCG pricing; no B2B custom pricing published.

positioning: Legacy agro-allied FMCG leader under the tagline 'Feeding and Enriching Lives Every Day,' spanning the full value chain from cultivation to consumer food brands.
strengths: 65+ years of operating history and strong household brand recognition (Golden Penny)
Full value-chain integration from agricultural inputs to consumer-facing food products
Diversified product range (flour, pasta, noodles, oils, sugar) spreads demand risk across categories
gaps_or_weaknesses: Recent delisting (Jan 2025) reduces public financial transparency going forward
Legacy FMCG brand positioning may read as less innovation-forward than newer entrants
perceived_target_customer: Mass-market Nigerian and West African consumers of staple food products, plus B2B buyers of flour and agro-allied inputs.
confidence_notes: Based on company site and Wikipedia; post-delisting financial detail is limited compared to when it was NSE-listed.
